Children.Text: VeloxDev is a suite of development tools for modern .NET, centered around Source Generators, which converges previously fragmented capabilities—such as MVVM, animation, theming, frame loops, AOP, Workflow, and Agent—into a unified abstraction. It advocates composition over inheritance, type safety, high extensibility, and elegant APIs.
Choose a GUI framework you prefer and install it; all packages reference VeloxDev.Core, and VeloxDev.Core.Extension is optional.

Cells: Annotate fields with [VeloxProperty] and methods with [VeloxCommand] to auto-generate properties and asynchronous commands
State interpolation animations: Snapshot recording, Fluent composition, cross-platform scheduling
[ThemeConfig] declares multiple themes; supports gradual switching and runtime overriding
Update / FixedUpdate frame loops; global time flow and pausing
Runtime proxying: before/after/override hooks for properties and methods
Generates reflection-preserving code for specified types, enabling trimming and NativeAOT compatibility
Tree / Node / Slot / Link + Helper graph-structured workflow core
Zero-dependency reflection toolkit + MAF Function Calling toolkit

Children.Text: If the member is derived from `INotifyCollectionChanged`, additional callback methods are provided. Specifically, when the value of a notifying property changes, subscription to and disposal of the callback methods are handled automatically internally. Note that partial properties cannot declare initial values at definition time; therefore, we recommend initializing these collections during construction.
The following code will all ultimately generate a `SaveCommand`, regardless of whether it carries the “Async” suffix.

No matter how you execute the command, the underlying mechanism is always waiting on a `Task`, so in most cases you don’t need to worry that calling `Execute` instead of `ExecuteAsync` will block the UI—they are nearly equivalent.

await SaveCommand.ExecuteAsync(null);

Children.Text: Almost any GUI framework provides proprietary interfaces for handling animations, which makes the creation of interpolation-based animations somewhat dependent on the GUI framework’s syntactic design—and may impose limitations. Take WPF as an example: standard animations must be built upon `DependencyProperty`. Although this design choice is intended to facilitate internal performance optimization, it also sacrifices flexibility.

VeloxDev advocates compatibility and internally designs a complete abstraction layer for animations—from construction to execution—so that each GUI framework requires only minimal adaptation code, and ultimately animations operate on standard properties rather than any GUI-specific objects.
Theme — Vivid Theme Color Switching Implementation
You can use the same API to build animations in any supported GUI framework—we provide the most elegant approach to date, with strong emphasis on type safety and thread safety.
In the following example, you will create an animation to modify the horizontal offset and fill color of a Rectangle element.
The animation process can be split into multiple segments and then connected sequentially.

Children.Code:
Transition<Rectangle>.Create()
    .Property()
    .Effect()
    .Then()  // Chain: execute the next animation immediately
    .Property()
    .Effect()
    .AwaitThen() // Chain: wait for a specified duration before executing the next animation
    .Property()
    .Effect();

Children.Text: Use the following code to control the animation toggle.

Children.Code:
animation.Execute(rect); // Start the animation on the rect instance

Transition.Exit(rect); // Terminate

Children.Text: Animation startup is thread-safe; you need not worry about the following scenario.

The `IInterpolable` interface allows types to participate directly in interpolation. By implementing this interface, your custom types can be used directly with the `Property` function when building animations in Fluent.
The `IEaseCalculator` interface allows you to customize easing effects.

Animations are based on interpolation between two states; the snapshot mechanism is one of its implementations. VeloxDev exposes this mechanism, so you can use it to create animations—or for state saving and restoration (used only in animation scenarios).

Children.Code:
// The essence is still animation; effects can be configured.
snapshot.Effect(TransitionEffects.Empty).Execute(view);

Workflow orchestration and automation are common in modern software—especially in business pipelines and device control flows. Today, you can elegantly build extensible, runtime workflows in .NET. Moreover, with advances in AI, it’s no longer far-fetched for agents to understand and manipulate in-memory objects. What you need is creativity and approval—not extensive coding.
Avalonia / WPF / WinUI / MAUI / WinForms have all implemented the following effect.
Based on MVVM, with no GUI dependencies

You can draw interactive workflow orchestration views in your preferred GUI framework, or run orchestrated workflows on UI-less servers. VeloxDev.Core.Extension provides persistence services to support sharing the same data across these modes.
Implements spatial indexing and querying of nodes using a grid-based hashing algorithm; real-world testing shows microsecond-level response times.
The workflow framework has deeply integrated the Function Calling capability provided by MAF, enabling your agent to understand and manipulate workflows at runtime.
WorkflowBuilder provides source code generation capabilities; simply annotate this feature to implement the workflow component core without inheritance.

WorkflowHelper provides the logical code required by workflow components. This code is injected into the view model during the generation phase and supports dynamic replacement, enabling a highly extensible architecture by overriding built-in Helper methods and subscribing to built-in Helper events.
A workflow is a graph structure containing four key components. This chapter will show you how to build the corresponding view models for these components.

Cells: Global container that stores all data of a single workflow
Node that hosts configuration and business logic execution
Connector that hosts connection relationships and channel direction constraints
Connection line that can access the source and destination Slot

In the definition phase of the node view model, there are two special members:  
- A class derived from `IWorkflowSlotViewModel`, which must be annotated with `[VeloxProperty]`.  
- A `SlotEnumerator<T>`, which can use `bool` or `enum` as conditional branches; internally, the collection generates multiple slots based on the condition and must also be annotated with `[VeloxProperty]`.  

With this setup, you can later render multiple connectors directly in the view using the `InputSlot` property and the `OutputSlots` collection. Note that the UI is responsible for tracking each slot’s coordinates (Anchor) and passing them back to the view model. If the UI lacks such coordinate tracking, the start and end positions of your `Link` component cannot be computed correctly.

The `SlotEnumerator<T>` implements the `IEnumerable` interface, with the following child elements defined:  
- `Name`: the name of the enumeration member  
- `Value`: the enumeration value  
- `Slot`: the automatically constructed `Slot` component

This chapter addresses the following question: How to implement a workflow GUI based on attached properties.

This section applies to Avalonia / WPF / WinUI / MAUI (WinForms has no attached property mechanism; although its API is similar, the recommended practice is to directly port from the repository’s demo).

Children.Code: <UserControl xmlns:behaviors="using:VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem.AttachedBehaviors" />

Children.Text: Provides behavior registration based on Name at the UserControl level, covering interaction behaviors required by elements such as Canvas, ScrollViewer, and grid adornment lines.

| Additional Property | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `IsEnabled` | `bool` | Enables the entire workflow canvas interaction behavior |
| `ScrollViewerName` | `string?` | Specifies the `ScrollViewer` used for scrolling and viewport calculations |
| `CanvasName` | `string?` | Specifies the workflow content canvas |
| `GridDecoratorName` | `string?` | Specifies the grid/ruler decorator used to synchronize scroll offset and content offset |
| `PointerPressSourceName` | `string?` | Specifies the element that receives pointer press events and initiates canvas panning |
Provides an object pooling mechanism for Canvas; reusing view elements plays a critical role in reducing GC pressure and improving rendering performance.

Cells: Interface for GUI framework-specific template selector

Children.Code:
<UserControl xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui"
			 xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
			 xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/expression/blend/2008"
			 xmlns:mc="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/markup-compatibility/2006"
			 mc:Ignorable="d" d:DesignWidth="800" d:DesignHeight="450"
			 x:Class="Demo.WorkflowView"
			 xmlns:local="using:Demo"
			 xmlns:vm="using:Demo.ViewModels"
			 xmlns:workflow="using:VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem"
			 x:DataType="vm:TreeViewModel"

             xmlns:behaviors="using:VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem.AttachedBehaviors"
             behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.IsEnabled="True"
             behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.ScrollViewerName="PART_ScrollViewer"
			 behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.CanvasName="PART_Canvas"
			 behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.GridDecoratorName="PART_GridDecorator"
			 behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.PointerPressSourceName="PART_SurfaceBorder" >

    <!-- Avalonia automatically matches templates placed here; no need to use ViewPool.TemplateSelector -->
    <!-- Whether RenderTransform needs binding depends on the specific GUI framework -->
    <UserControl.DataTemplates>
        <!-- Node template -->
		<DataTemplate DataType="vm:NodeViewModel">
			<local:NodeView Width="{Binding Size.Width}" Height="{Binding Size.Height}"
							Canvas.Left="{Binding Anchor.Horizontal}" Canvas.Top="{Binding Anchor.Vertical}" ZIndex="{Binding Anchor.Layer}"
							RenderTransform="{Binding $parent[local:WorkflowView].(behaviors:WorkflowCanvasTransformBehavior.Transform)}"/>
		</DataTemplate>

        <!-- Connection template -->
		<DataTemplate DataType="vm:LinkViewModel">
			<local:BezierCurveView StartLeft="{Binding Sender.Anchor.Horizontal}" StartTop="{Binding Sender.Anchor.Vertical}"
								   EndLeft="{Binding Receiver.Anchor.Horizontal}" EndTop="{Binding Receiver.Anchor.Vertical}"
                                   ZIndex="-1"
								   CanRender="{Binding IsVisible}"
								   Width="{Binding $parent[Canvas].Bounds.Width}" Height="{Binding $parent[Canvas].Bounds.Height}"
								   RenderTransform="{Binding $parent[local:WorkflowView].(behaviors:WorkflowCanvasTransformBehavior.Transform)}" />
		</DataTemplate>
    </UserControl.DataTemplates>

	<!-- Render node editor (with virtualization support) -->
	<Grid Grid.Column="2">
        <Border x:Name="PART_SurfaceBorder" BorderBrush="White" BorderThickness="1" CornerRadius="3">
            <local:WorkflowGridDecorator x:Name="PART_GridDecorator">
                <ScrollViewer x:Name="PART_ScrollViewer" HorizontalScrollBarVisibility="Auto" >
				    <Canvas x:Name="PART_Canvas"
                            x:DataType="vm:TreeViewModel" Width="{Binding Layout.ActualSize.Width}" Height="{Binding Layout.ActualSize.Height}" HorizontalAlignment="Left" VerticalAlignment="Top"
                            behaviors:ViewPool.ItemsSource="{Binding Helper.VisibleItems}">
					    <Canvas.DataTemplates>
						    <!-- Data templates are searched in order of priority: Self > Parent > App -->
					    </Canvas.DataTemplates>
				    </Canvas>
			    </ScrollViewer>
		    </local:WorkflowGridDecorator>
	    </Border>
	</Grid>

</UserControl>

VeloxDev.Core.Extension provides a set of extension methods for view models, with the following features:  
- `Dictionary<,>` supports any type as the key, no longer limited to `string`.  
- Only properties whose getter and setter are both `public` will be serialized, and serialization handles only properties.

| Target / Scenario | Configuration Syntax | Why This Configuration | Recommended? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Debugging, viewing JSON, performing diffs | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithIndented().WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto).WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | Maximizes JSON readability and fully preserves polymorphism, null values, and default values — ideal for diagnosing workflow restoration issues | Strongly recommended |
| Production saving to file | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithCompact().WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto).WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | Produces smaller files while retaining all information required for full workflow persistence and restoration | Recommended |
| Minimal JSON size only | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithCompact().WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Ignore).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Ignore)` | Reduces JSON size but may omit fields essential for restoration — unsuitable for complex workflow object graphs | Use with caution |
| Derived nodes, interface properties, or polymorphic objects | `WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto)` | Enables deserialization to restore the actual runtime type; typically required in Workflow scenarios | Required |
| Ensuring identical state after restoration | `WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | Preserves `null` values and default values, preventing state divergence post-deserialization | Recommended |
| Improving JSON readability only | `WithIndented()` | Improves human readability only; does not affect object semantics | As needed |
| Reducing file size only | `WithCompact()` | Removes indentation and whitespace — the simplest compression method | As needed |
| Synchronous API usage | `var json = tree.Serialize(options);` / `var tree = json.Deserialize<TreeViewModel>(options);` | Suitable for simple save/load operations | Recommended |
| Safe loading | `json.TryDeserialize<TreeViewModel>(options, out var tree)` | More robust when loading from external or user-provided files that may be corrupted — avoids throwing exceptions | Strongly recommended |
| Asynchronous API usage | `await tree.SerializeAsync(options)` / `await json.DeserializeAsync<TreeViewModel>(options)` | Suitable for UI, CLI, and file I/O pipelines | Recommended |
| Streaming / Stream API usage | `await tree.SerializeToStreamAsync(stream, options)` / `await stream.DeserializeFromStreamAsync<TreeViewModel>(options)` | Suitable for file streams, network streams, and memory streams | Recommended |
| Most robust default for Workflows | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithIndented().WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto).WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | Use this if unsure — safest for workflow object graphs | Default choice |

> **Target Framework**: netstandard2.0 and later +, platform adapters vary

---

## Overview

The transition system provides a framework-agnostic animation engine that supports property interpolation.  
This architecture separates concerns into:

```
TransitionEffect  ─► Defines timing, frame rate (FPS), looping, easing, and lifecycle events
IFrameState       ─► Stores target property values and interpolators
IFrameInterpolator─► Converts IFrameState into IFrameSequence (frame data)
IFrameSequence    ─► Applies animation frame-by-frame to the target object
ITransitionInterpreter ─► Drives the frame loop (timing, cancellation)
ITransitionScheduler   ─► Manages exclusivity and task lifecycles
IUIThreadInspector     ─► Abstracts UI thread marshaling (platform-specific)
```

Platform adapters (Avalonia, WPF, WinForms, MAUI, WinUI) provide concrete implementations of platform-specific types.

## 13. `TransitionCore` / `Transition<T>` — High-Level Streaming API

### Static Exit

```csharp
TransitionCore.Exit<T>(T target, bool IncludeMutual = true, bool IncludeNoMutual = false)
```
Stops running animations on `target`. `IncludeMutual` stops the mutually exclusive scheduler. `IncludeNoMutual` stops the parallel scheduler.

### `Transition<T>.StateSnapshot` — Fluent Builder

The platform adapter exposes a concrete `Transition<T>.StateSnapshot`. Core workflow:

```csharp
// 1. Capture current state
var snapshot = myControl.Snapshot(c => c.Opacity, c => c.Width);

// 2. Configure target state
snapshot
    .Set(c => c.Opacity, 1.0)
    .Set(c => c.Width, 200)
    .Effect(effect => { effect.Duration = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(0.3); effect.Ease = Eases.Cubic.Out; })
    .Execute(myControl);
```

**Extension methods** (in `TransitionCoreEx`):

```csharp
T Await<T>(this T snapshot, TimeSpan timeSpan)          // Insert a delay before the next segment
T Then<T>(this T snapshot)                               // Chain a new snapshot segment
T AwaitThen<T>(this T snapshot, TimeSpan timeSpan)      // Delay + chain
TSnapshot Interpolator<TSnapshot,TTarget,TValue>(
    this TSnapshot snapshot,
    Expression<Func<TTarget, TValue>> property,
    IValueInterpolator interpolator)                     // Override the interpolator for a single property
void Execute<T>(this T snapshot, object target, bool CanMutualTask = true)
void Execute<T>(this T snapshot, bool CanMutualTask = true)
```

**Platform Adapter** `TransitionEx` extension method:

```csharp
// Avalonia / WPF:
Transition<T>.StateSnapshot Snapshot<T>(this T target,
    params Expression<Func<T, object?>>[] expressions)          // Capture specific properties
Transition<T>.StateSnapshot SnapshotAll<T>(this T target,
    params Expression<Func<T, object?>>[] extraExpressions)     // Capture all interpolatable properties
Transition<T>.StateSnapshot SnapshotExcept<T>(this T target,
    params Expression<Func<T, object?>>[] excludedExpressions)  // Capture all properties except the listed ones
```

## Overview

The Workflow System is the core module of VeloxDev. It provides a node-based visual programming model composed of four main ViewModel components:

| Component | Interface | Default Implementation |
|-----------|-----------|----------------------|
| Tree (Workspace) | `IWorkflowTreeViewModel` | `TreeViewModelBase` |
| Node | `IWorkflowNodeViewModel` | `NodeViewModelBase` |
| Slot (Port) | `IWorkflowSlotViewModel` | `SlotViewModelBase` |
| Link (Connection) | `IWorkflowLinkViewModel` | `LinkViewModelBase` |

Each component is paired with a **Helper** interface, which encapsulates its internal logic and thus enables full replacement via `SetHelper(...)`.

---

**Namespace**: `VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem`  
**Inherits from**: `INotifyPropertyChanging`, `INotifyPropertyChanged`

```
│       Initializes this component. Called automatically by the constructor.

│       Raises INotifyPropertyChanged to notify the UI after a property changes.
└── IVeloxCommand CloseCommand { get; }
        Terminates all running tasks on this component. Parameter: null.
```

Based on MAF’s Function Calling, the repository starts from C# objects and progressively provides agent-ready tools, ultimately evolving into workflow systems and other functionality modules suitable for agent empowerment. In the end, developers only need to annotate members in their code using natural language; the agent can then dynamically understand and dynamically modify in-memory objects at runtime.

# VeloxDev Agent Core Capabilities Summary

## Core Positioning
- Define the agent metadata representation format
- Provides multilingual contextual description capability
- Provide reflection discovery capability
- Provides access to and execution of properties, methods, and commands
- Wrap any .NET object as an AI-callable set of tools

## Core Modules
### Metadata and Language Systems
- Location: `Src/Core/VeloxDev.Core/AI/`
- Core types:  
  - `AgentContextAttribute`: Provides semantic descriptions for types, properties, methods, etc.  
  - `AgentCommandParameterAttribute`: Marks the expected type of a command parameter.  
  - `AgentLanguages`: An enumeration of supported languages.  
  - Purpose: Provides a unified descriptive language to achieve “interpretability”.

### Context Reading
- `AgentContextReader`: Extracts semantic descriptions from code

### Property Access
- `AgentPropertyAccessor`:
  - Discover, read, and set properties
  - Set properties in batches
  - Basic type conversion

### Method Call
- `AgentMethodInvoker`:
  - Discovers public methods and invokes them
  - Handles overload resolution
  - Performs parameter type conversion
  - Supports both instance and static methods

### Command Discovery and Execution
- `AgentCommandDiscoverer`:
  - Discovers `ICommand` properties on objects
  - Reads command parameters and descriptions
  - Executes commands by name
  - Especially suitable for MVVM-style objects

### Type Parsing
- `AgentTypeResolver`: Resolves .NET types in loaded assemblies

### Tool Call Notification
- `AgentToolCallEventArgs`: Tool call event data
- `IAgentToolCallNotifier`: Tool call notification interface

## Generic Object Utilities
- Location: `Src/Core/VeloxDev.Core.Extension/Agent/AgentObjectToolkit.cs`
- Core types:  
  - `AgentObjectToolkit`: Wraps any object into a set of `AITool`s  
  - Exposable tool capabilities:  
    - Retrieve component information  
    - List/Get/Set properties  
    - List/Execute commands  
    - List/Invoke methods  
    - Resolve types
- Value:  
  - No requirement for a specific framework or base class  
  - Unified reflection capability for AI tools

## Capability Layering
| Level | Content |
|---|---|
| Metadata Layer | Descriptive attributes, language support |
| Discovery Layer | Context reading, property/method/command discovery |
| Execution Layer | Property setting, method invocation, command execution |
| Tooling Layer | Object wrapping, tooling |

## Features
- **Highly generic**: Targets arbitrary .NET objects and is not tied to any specific framework
- **Loose Coupling**: Metadata is separated from execution logic
- **Easy to extend**: Supports extending new tool wrappers, descriptors, etc.

## Applicable Scenarios
- ViewModel Agentization
- Generic component debugging
- AI-assisted object inspection
- Reflection Tool System Construction
- Multilingual Semantic-Driven Agent

## Core Values
- Make the code structure understandable by agents
- Enable object capabilities to be discoverable by Agents
- Make runtime objects safely callable by Agents
- Enable any object to be uniformly wrapped as an AI toolset
Wrap a plain object to make it operable by an Agent.

# VeloxDev Workflow Capabilities Summary

## Core Positioning
For visual workflow editing scenarios, provides:
- Workflow diagram data model
- Node layout and space management
- Connection, broadcasting, reverse broadcasting, and other runtime behaviors
- Edit history with undo/redo support
- Dynamic nodes, dynamic slots, and enumerated slot management
- Agent-oriented workflow query, operation, analysis, and takeover capabilities

## Core Domain Model
### Core Interfaces
- `IWorkflowViewModel`: Generic base interface for the Workflow component
- `IWorkflowTreeViewModel`: Workflow tree/canvas root object
- `IWorkflowNodeViewModel`: Workflow node object
- `IWorkflowSlotViewModel`: Workflow slot object
- `IWorkflowLinkViewModel`: Workflow link object
- Helper interface: Provides behavior-driven assistance

### Core Data Types
- `Anchor`: Node position and hierarchy
- `Offset`/`Size`/`Viewport`: Spatial information
- `WorkflowActionPair`: Undo/Redo action pair
- `CellKey`/`CanvasLayout`: Layout and Grid

### Default Implementation Template
- `TreeViewModelBase`: Default implementation of the workflow tree
- `NodeViewModelBase`: Default node implementation
- `SlotViewModelBase`/`LinkViewModelBase`: Default implementations for slots and links

## Basic runtime capabilities
### Tree-level capabilities
- Maintain the node collection `Nodes` and the link collection `Links`
- Maintain the connection mapping `LinksMap`
- Create a node and set the pointer position
- Initiate and receive connection establishment
- Supports undo and redo

### Node-level capabilities
- Maintain the `Anchor`, `Size`, and `Slots` nodes
- Provides commands for moving, setting position/size
- Provides commands to create slots and delete nodes
- Provides commands to execute jobs, broadcast, and reverse broadcast

### Slot and Link Capabilities
- Slot: Input/output connection points on a node
- Link: The actual connection between two slots
- The Tree rapidly maintains the topology mapping via `LinksMap`.

## Extension Mechanism
### Standard Extension Modules
- `WorkflowTreeEx`/`WorkflowNodeEx`, etc.: Convenience APIs supplementing the default workflow components

### Template Helper Module
- `TreeHelper`/`NodeHelper`, etc.: Contain the behavioral logic in the default implementation
- Includes: node creation, connection establishment, deletion logic, Undo/Redo commit

### Spatial and Visualization Support
- `WorkflowSpatialManager`: Spatial Index
- `SpatialGridHashMap`: View Frustum Query
- Supports node collision detection and boundary calculation

## Agent operation support
### Entry Points and Scopes
- `AgentEx`: Provides the `AsAgentScope()` entry point
- `WorkflowAgentScope`: Manages agent scope, context, and resources
- Supports registration of custom components, tools, and skills

### Type Context Capability
- `AgentContextCollector`: Generates agent-readable context documentation for the Workflow type
- `AgentEmbeddedResources`: Reads embedded Markdown skills and reference materials

### State Tracking Capability
- `WorkflowStateTracker`: Generates workflow snapshots and outputs differences
- Supports calculating added/removed/modified nodes and connections
- Suitable for agents to maintain state awareness in multi-turn conversations

### Parameter Protocol Model
- `WorkflowProtocols.cs`: Provides strongly-typed protocol objects
- Includes: `MoveNodeProtocol`, `ConnectSlotsProtocol`, `CreateNodeProtocol`, etc.
- Enable strong-typed expression for Workflow action parameters

## WorkflowAgentToolkit Capabilities
### Query Capabilities
- `ListNodes`/`GetNodeDetail`: Query node information
- `ListConnections`/`GetFullTopology`: Query the connection topology
- `GetWorkflowSummary`/`GetComponentContext`: Retrieve the summary and context

### Change-class Capabilities
- `MoveNode`/`SetNodePosition`/`ResizeNode`: Adjust node position and size
- `DeleteNode`/`CreateNode`: Node creation and deletion
- `PatchNodeProperties`: Batch modify properties

### Connection Management Capabilities
- `ConnectSlots`/`DisconnectSlots`: Connect and disconnect slots
- `DisconnectAllFromNode`/`ReplaceConnection`: Batch connection management
- `ConnectByProperty`: Connect by property

### Execution-Class Capabilities
- `ExecuteWork`: Execute node work
- `BroadcastNode`/`ReverseBroadcastNode`: Broadcast and Reverse Broadcast
- `ExecuteCommandOnNode`: Execute command on node
- `BatchExecute`: Batch execution

### Slot Collection and Enumerating Slot Capabilities
- `ListSlotProperties`: List slot properties
- `AddSlotToCollection`/`RemoveSlotFromCollection`: Dynamic slot management
- `SetEnumSlotCollection`/`ConnectEnumSlot`: Enum slot management

### Search, Analysis, and Layout Capabilities
- `FindNodes`/`SearchForward`/`SearchReverse`: Topological search
- `FindPath`/`IsConnected`: Path finding
- `GetNodeStatistics`/`ValidateWorkflow`: Statistical Analysis
- `AlignNodes`/`AutoLayout`/`ArrangeNodes`: Automatic layout

### History and Status Capabilities
- `TakeSnapshot`/`GetChangesSinceSnapshot`: state snapshot
- `Undo`/`Redo`: Edit rollback

## Capabilities
1. **Graph-oriented**: Focuses on node sets, edge topology, and propagation relationships
2. **Editor-oriented scenarios**: Supports visual editing, spatial navigation, and Undo/Redo
3. **Extensible and Customizable**: Supports custom node types, slot types, and enumerated slots

## Applicable Scenarios
- Visual workflow editor
- Node-based logic orchestration system
- Data Flow / Control Flow Diagram Designer
- Rule Orchestration and Execution Graph System
- Workflow scenarios requiring Agent takeover or assisted editing

## Core Values
- Define a unified workflow component model
- Provide default implementation and behavior abstraction layer
- Supports topology connections, spatial layout, and historical rollback
- Supports advanced structures such as dynamic slots and enumerated slots
- Supports querying, operating, and fully taking over agent-oriented workflows
